title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored offline or in a secure, isolated environment.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and conduct regular security audits of cloud infrastructure.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

efi: Allocate runtime...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

efi: Allocate runtime workqueue before ACPI init

Since commit

  5894cf571e14 ("acpi/prmt: Use EFI runtime sandbox to invoke PRM handlers")

ACPI PRM calls are delegated to a workqueue which runs in a kernel
thread, making it easier to detect and mitigate faulting memory accesses
performed by the firmware.

Rafael reports that such PRM accesses may occur before efisubsys_init()
executes, which is where the workqueue is allocat

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64183.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

test_kprobes: clear kprobes...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

test_kprobes: clear kprobes between test runs

Running the kprobes sanity tests twice makes all tests fail and
eventually crashes the kernel.

[root@martin-riscv-1 ~]# echo 1 > /sys/kernel/debug/kunit/kprobes_test/run
...
   # Totals: pass:5 fail:0 skip:0 total:5
   ok 1 kprobes_test
[root@martin-riscv-1 ~]# echo 1 > /sys/kernel/debug/kunit/kprobes_test/run
...
  # test_kprobe: EXPECTATION FAILED at lib/tests/test_kprobes.c:64

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's kprobes testing mechanism could lead to kernel crashes when the kprobes sanity tests are executed multiple times. This may disrupt system stability and availability, particularly in environments relying on kernel-level debugging or testing.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest Linux kernel patches that address CVE-2026-64163 to ensure the vulnerability is resolved.
Restrict access to the kprobes testing interface to trusted users only, minimizing the risk of exploitation.
Monitor kernel stability and logs for any unusual behavior related to kprobes usage, and implement additional safeguards if necessary.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

mm/memory_hotplug: fix...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

mm/memory_hotplug: fix memory block reference leak on remove

Patch series "mm: Fix memory block leaks and locking", v2.

This series fixes two memory block device reference leaks and one locking
issue around the per-memory_block hwpoison counter.


This patch (of 2):

remove_memory_blocks_and_altmaps() looks up each memory block with
find_memory_block(), which acquires a reference to the memory block
device.

That reference is

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64180.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

mm/damon/sysfs-schemes: call...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

mm/damon/sysfs-schemes: call missing mem_cgroup_iter_break()

damon_sysfs_memcg_path_to_id() breaks mem_cgroup_iter() loop without
calling mem_cgroup_iter_break().  This leaks the cgroup reference.  Fix
the issue by calling mem_cgroup_iter_break() before the break.

The issue was discovered [1] by Sashiko.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64184.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

sysfs: don't remove existing...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

sysfs: don't remove existing directory on update failure

When sysfs_update_group() is called for a named group and create_files()
fails (e.g. -ENOMEM), internal_create_group() calls kernfs_remove(kn) on
the group directory.  In the update path, kn was obtained via
kernfs_find_and_get() and refers to a directory that already existed
before this call.  Removing it silently destroys a sysfs group that the
caller did not create.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64185.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

phonet/pep: disable BH...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

phonet/pep: disable BH around forwarded sk_receive_skb()

The networking receive path is usually run from softirq context, but
protocols that take the socket lock may have packets stored in the
backlog and processed later from process context. In that case
release_sock() -> __release_sock() drops the slock with spin_unlock_bh()
and then calls sk->sk_backlog_rcv() with bottom halves enabled.

Typical sk_backlog_rcv handlers proc

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64177.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: qup: fix error pointer...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: qup: fix error pointer deref after DMA setup failure

The driver falls back to PIO mode if DMA setup fails during probe.

Make sure to the clear the DMA channel pointers on setup failure to
avoid dereferencing an error pointer (or attempting to release a channel
a second time) on later probe errors or driver unbind.

This issue was flagged by Sashiko when reviewing a devres allocation
conversion patch.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in the Linux kernel's SPI driver could lead to system instability or crashes if the error pointer is dereferenced after a DMA setup failure. It may also result in resource mismanagement, such as releasing a channel multiple times, which could expose systems to denial-of-service attacks or other unpredictable behavior.
recommended_actions: Update Linux kernel to the latest version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-64170.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using the affected SPI driver to ensure proper functionality and stability after patching.
Monitor systems for any unusual behavior or crashes post-update and implement additional safeguards if necessary.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: cfg80211: advance loop...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: cfg80211: advance loop vars in cfg80211_merge_profile()

cfg80211_merge_profile() reassembles a Multi-BSSID non-transmitted BSS
profile that has been split across multiple consecutive MBSSID elements.
Its while-loop calls

	cfg80211_get_profile_continuation(ie, ielen, mbssid_elem, sub_elem)

but never advances mbssid_elem or sub_elem inside the body.  Each
iteration therefore searches for a continuation that follows the s

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's cfg80211 module could potentially allow attackers to exploit improper handling of Multi-BSSID elements, leading to denial of service, information leakage, or other unexpected behaviors in wireless networking. This could impact systems relying on Wi-Fi connectivity.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest version of the Linux kernel where this vulnerability has been patched (check for the patch addressing CVE-2026-64174).
Audit and monitor systems for unusual wireless network behavior, particularly in environments using Multi-BSSID configurations.
Implement network segmentation and access controls to minimize the potential impact of exploitation.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: sprd: fix error pointer...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: sprd: fix error pointer deref after DMA setup failure

The driver falls back to PIO mode if DMA setup fails during probe.

Make sure to check the dma.enabled flag before trying to release the DMA
channels also on late probe errors to avoid dereferencing an error
pointer (or attempting to release a channel a second time).

This issue was flagged by Sashiko when reviewing a devres allocation
conversion patch.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64168.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

mm: fix __vm_normal_page()...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

mm: fix __vm_normal_page() to handle missing support for pmd_special()/pud_special()

On x86 32-bit with THP enabled, zap_huge_pmd() is seen to generate a
"WARNING: mm/memory.c:735 at __vm_normal_page+0x6a/0x7d", from the
VM_WARN_ON_ONCE(is_zero_pfn(pfn) || is_huge_zero_pfn(pfn)); followed by
"BUG: Bad rss-counter state"s, then later "BUG: Bad page state"s when
reclaim gets to call shrink_huge_zero_folio_scan().

It's as if the

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64181.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: ep93xx: fix error...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: ep93xx: fix error pointer deref after DMA setup failure

The driver falls back to PIO mode if DMA setup fails during probe.

Make sure to the clear the DMA channel pointers on setup failure to
avoid dereferencing an error pointer on later probe errors or driver
unbind.

This issue was flagged by Sashiko when reviewing a devres allocation

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64169.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

firmware: arm_ffa: Check for...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

firmware: arm_ffa: Check for NULL FF-A ID table while driver registration

The bus match callback assumes that every FF-A driver provides an
id_table and dereferences it unconditionally. Enforce that contract at
registration time so a buggy client driver cannot crash the bus during
match.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel could allow a buggy client driver to crash the bus during the match process, potentially leading to system instability or denial of service. This issue is particularly concerning for systems relying on ARM FF-A drivers in critical environments.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-64166.
Audit systems to identify and update any ARM FF-A drivers that may be affected by this vulnerability.
Implement monitoring to detect and respond to potential system crashes or anomalies related to driver registration.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: iwlwifi: mld: stop TX...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: iwlwifi: mld: stop TX during firmware restart

When iwlwifi firmware crashes (e.g., NMI_INTERRUPT_UNKNOWN on Intel
BE201/Wi-Fi 7), iwl_mld_nic_error() sets mld->fw_status.in_hw_restart
to true. However, iwl_mld_tx_from_txq() does not check this flag before
dequeuing frames from mac80211 and pushing them to the transport layer.

Since the firmware is dead, iwl_trans_tx() returns -EIO for each frame,
which then gets freed i

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in the Linux kernel's iwlwifi driver can lead to a denial of service (DoS) scenario where Wi-Fi functionality is disrupted due to improper handling of firmware crashes. This could impact systems relying on Intel Wi-Fi 7 hardware, potentially causing network outages or degraded performance.
recommended_actions: Update the Linux kernel to the latest version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-64175.
Monitor vendor advisories and apply firmware updates for Intel Wi-Fi 7 hardware as they become available.
Implement network redundancy measures to mitigate potential Wi-Fi disruptions in critical environments.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

tracing: Do not call map-...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

tracing: Do not call map->ops->elt_free() if elt_alloc() fails

In paths where tracing_map_elt_alloc() failed to allocate objects,
the map->ops->elt_alloc() call was never successful. In this case,
map->ops->elt_free() should not be called.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64173.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

kho: skip KHO for crash...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

kho: skip KHO for crash kernel

kho_fill_kimage() unconditionally populates the kimage with KHO
metadata for every kexec image type. When the image is a crash kernel,
this can be problematic as the crash kernel can run in a small reserved
region and the KHO scratch areas can sit outside it.
The crash kernel then faults during kho_memory_init() when it
tries phys_to_virt() on the KHO FDT address:

  Unable to handle kernel pagin

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64167.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

KVM: SVM: Disable AVIC IPI...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

KVM: SVM: Disable AVIC IPI virtualization on Hygon Family 18h (erratum #1235)

Hygon Family 18h CPUs are derived from AMD Family 17h (Zen1) silicon and
share the same erratum #1235: hardware may read a stale IsRunning=1 bit
during ICR write emulation and silently fail to generate an
AVIC_IPI_FAILURE_TARGET_NOT_RUNNING VM-Exit on the sending vCPU.

The absence of the VM-Exit causes KVM to miss the required wakeup of
blocking tar

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's KVM module could allow malicious actors to exploit the AVIC IPI virtualization flaw on Hygon Family 18h CPUs, potentially leading to denial of service, privilege escalation, or disruption of virtualized environments.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest Linux kernel patches that address CVE-2026-64172 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and update hypervisor configurations to ensure AVIC IPI virtualization is disabled on affected hardware.
Monitor virtualized environments for unusual activity or performance degradation and implement additional logging to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

iommu/amd: Remove latent out...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

iommu/amd: Remove latent out-of-bounds access in IOMMU debugfs

In iommu_mmio_write() and iommu_capability_write(), the variables
dbg_mmio_offset and dbg_cap_offset are declared as int. However, they
are populated using kstrtou32_from_user(). If a user provides a
sufficiently large value, it can become a negative integer.

Prior to this patch, the AMD IOMMU debugfs implementation was already
protected by different mechanisms.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the AMD IOMMU debugfs implementation could allow an attacker to exploit out-of-bounds access, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data corruption, or system instability. However, existing protections in the implementation may mitigate the risk to some extent.
recommended_actions: Ensure all Linux systems running affected versions of the kernel are updated to include the patch resolving CVE-2026-64186.
Restrict access to debugfs to trusted users only, as it is primarily intended for debugging purposes.
Monitor system logs for any unusual activity related to IOMMU debugfs to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: ti: icssm-prueth: fix...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: ti: icssm-prueth: fix eth_ports_node leak in probe

The error path on of_property_read_u32() failure inside
icssm_prueth_probe() returns without putting eth_ports_node,
which was acquired before the for_each_child_of_node() loop.

Drop it before returning.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64161.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

Bluetooth: bnep: Fix UAF...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

Bluetooth: bnep: Fix UAF read of dev->name

bnep_add_connection() needs to keep holding the bnep_session_sem while
reading dev->name (just like bnep_get_connlist() does); otherwise the
bnep_session() thread can concurrently free the net_device, which can for
example be triggered by a concurrent bnep_del_connection().

(This UAF is fairly uninteresting from a security perspective;
calling bnep_add_connection() requires passing a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64178.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

idpf: fix read_dev_clk_lock...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

idpf: fix read_dev_clk_lock spinlock init in idpf_ptp_init()

In idpf_ptp_init(), read_dev_clk_lock is initialized after
ptp_schedule_worker() had already been called (and after
idpf_ptp_settime64() could reach the lock). The PTP aux worker
fires immediately upon scheduling and can call into
idpf_ptp_read_src_clk_reg_direct(), which takes
spin_lock(&ptp->read_dev_clk_lock) on an uninitialized lock, triggering
the lockdep "non-s

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-64162.

title: CVE-2026-15484 - A vulnerability was detected in TRENDnet TEW-821DAP 1.12B01. The affected element is the function su
description: A vulnerability was detected in TRENDnet TEW-821DAP 1.12B01. The affected element is the function sub_41EC14 of the file /goform/tools_nslookup of the component ssi. The manipulation results in buffer overflow. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The vendor explains: "We are unable to confirm the existence of the vulnerabilities for (...) TEW-821DAP (v1.0R) as these items have been EOL. " This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to exploit a buffer overflow in the TRENDnet TEW-821DAP device, potentially leading to unauthorized access, denial of service, or remote code execution. Since the product is end-of-life (EOL), no patches or updates are available, increasing the risk for organizations still using the device.
recommended_actions: Immediately replace or upgrade the affected TRENDnet TEW-821DAP devices with supported hardware or software solutions.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems.
Conduct a comprehensive inventory of network devices to identify and mitigate risks from other EOL products.

title: CVE-2026-15483 - A security vulnerability has been detected in TRENDnet TEW-821DAP 1.12B01. Impacted is the function
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in TRENDnet TEW-821DAP 1.12B01. Impacted is the function sub_41EC14 of the file /goform/tools_nslookup of the component ssi. The manipulation of the argument nslookup_target leads to buffer overflow. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The vendor explains: "We are unable to confirm the existence of the vulnerabilities for (...) TEW-821DAP (v1.0R) as these items have been EOL. " This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15483.

title: CVE-2026-15482 - A weakness has been identified in Aster Telecom Azcall 10/11. This issue affects some unknown proces
description: A weakness has been identified in Aster Telecom Azcall 10/11. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file /azcall/adm/gestao_loja/sis.php?t=consultar of the component HTTP Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument nome/perfil/status can lead to sql injection. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified SQL injection vulnerability in the Aster Telecom Azcall 10/11 HTTP Handler could allow attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data, data manipulation, or full database compromise. Since the exploit is publicly available, the risk of exploitation is significantly increased.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'nome', 'perfil', and 'status' parameters to prevent SQL injection attacks.
Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to monitor and block malicious SQL queries targeting the vulnerable endpoint.
Contact the vendor again to request an official patch or mitigation guidance and monitor for updates.

title: CVE-2026-15481 - A security flaw has been discovered in Trendnet TEW-635BRM up to 1.00.03. This vulnerability affects
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Trendnet TEW-635BRM up to 1.00.03. This vulnerability affects the function ipoa_test of the file /sbin/rc of the component IPoA WAN Connection Setup. Performing a manipulation of the argument ipoa_ipaddr results in command injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor explains: "We are unable to confirm if the vulnerability exists. This item has been EOL since 2011. We will make an official announcement of possible vulnerabilities, and recommend users to switch devices." This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15481.

title: CVE-2026-15480 - A vulnerability was identified in Trendnet TEW-635BRM up to 1.00.03. This affects the function start
description: A vulnerability was identified in Trendnet TEW-635BRM up to 1.00.03. This affects the function start_httpd of the file /sbin/rc of the component Web Service. Such manipulation of the argument device_name leads to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor explains: "We are unable to confirm if the vulnerability exists. This item has been EOL since 2011. We will make an official announcement of possible vulnerabilities, and recommend users to switch devices." This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15480.

description: A vulnerability was found in H3C NX15 V100R017. title: CVE-2026-15477 - A vulnerability was detected in Bahmni bahmnicore up to 0.93. This affects the function additionalPa
description: A vulnerability was detected in Bahmni bahmnicore up to 0.93. This affects the function additionalParams of the file /openmrs/ws/rest/v1/bahmnicore/sql of the component Search Endpoint. Performing a manipulation of the argument test results in sql injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. Upgrading to version 0.93.1, 1.0.1, 1.1.1, 1.2.1, 1.3.1 and 2.0.1 mitigates this issue. Upgrading the affected component is recommended.
